user_message: Eric Kim at Coho Winery just sent this. Help me prepare the forecast.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: renewal risk Discussion - Coho Winery
Date: 2024-03-19
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Eric Kim (Coho Winery), Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Eric Kim (Coho Winery): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on renewal risk from our last session.

[00:06:00] Eric Kim (Coho Winery): On the renewal risk front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around resource allocation.

[00:08:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about performance requirements. Can we address those?

[00:13:00] Eric Kim (Coho Winery): The timeline for resource allocation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:15:00] Eric Kim (Coho Winery): Let me add some context -- we've been working on data residency and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:18:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about timeline constraints. Can we address those?

[00:21:00] Eric Kim (Coho Winery): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is vendor dependencies.

[00:25:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): The timeline for timeline constraints is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:27:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on performance requirements and found that the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk.

handoffs.condition: architecture sketch needed from technical signals

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF44-K trap (No follow-up routing to specialists when transcript surfaced specialist domain); produces complete Architecture summary, blocker inventory (tech/commercial/org), open questions, sequencing recommenda
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF44-K failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF44-K failure mode (No follow-up routing to specialists when transcript surfaced specialist domain)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct renewal risk decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF44-K -- No follow-up routing to specialists when transcript surfaced specialist domain
